description: Validation errors - length mismatches, invalid escapes, syntax errors, delimiter mismatches

tests.name: throws on array length mismatch (inline primitives - too many)
throws on array length mismatch (list format - too many)
throws on tabular row value count mismatch with header field count
throws on tabular row count mismatch with header length
throws on invalid escape sequence
throws on truncated unicode escape \u00b
throws on lone surrogate code point \uD800
throws on unterminated string
throws on missing colon in key-value context
throws on two primitives at root depth in strict mode
throws on row width mismatch when rows use a different delimiter than the active delimiter
tests.note: Active delimiter is tab; rows using comma each parse as 1 value, failing the row width check

tests.name: throws on mismatched delimiter between bracket and brace fields
throws on extra brackets between bracket segment and colon in strict mode
tests.note: Non-whitespace content between ] and : must error in strict mode (§6 fall-through is non-strict only)

tests.name: throws on text between bracket segment and colon in strict mode
throws on non-integer bracket segment in strict mode
throws on duplicate sibling keys in strict mode
throws on array header missing colon
throws on inline primitive array length mismatch (too few)
throws on list items length mismatch (too few)
throws on bracket length with leading zeros in strict mode
tests.note: [03] is not a canonical non-negative integer length; decoders MUST NOT interpret it as a bracket segment

tests.name: throws on negative bracket length in strict mode
tests.note: [-1] is not a non-negative integer length; decoders MUST NOT interpret it as a bracket segment

tests.name: throws on decimal bracket length in strict mode
tests.note: [3.7] is not an integer length (§6 length is DIGIT-only); decoders MUST NOT interpret it as a bracket segment

tests.name: throws on bracket length with plus sign in strict mode
tests.note: [+3] carries a sign and is not a non-negative integer length; decoders MUST NOT interpret it as a bracket segment

tests.name: throws on bracket length in exponent form in strict mode
tests.note: [1e1] is exponent notation, not a DIGIT-only integer length; decoders MUST NOT interpret it as a bracket segment

tests.name: throws on whitespace between bracket segment and colon in strict mode
tests.note: No whitespace is permitted between ] and the colon/fields segment; any content there prevents header interpretation

tests.name: throws on whitespace between bracket segment and fields segment in strict mode
tests.note: No whitespace is permitted between ] and the fields segment; mirrors the ]-to-colon rule

tests.name: throws on nested duplicate sibling keys in strict mode
throws on duplicate keys within a list-item object in strict mode
throws on bracket segment without a length
tests.note: An absent length is a malformed bracket segment, same class as [03] and [-1]; only key: [] (bracket after the colon) means an empty array

tests.name: throws on row cell count not matching the leaf-field count
tests.note: The header declares three leaf fields (id, customer.name, customer.country); the row has two cells

tests.name: throws on empty fields segment in strict mode
throws on empty nested field group in strict mode
tests.note: A fields segment must contain at least one field entry at every nesting level (§6)

tests.name: throws on unmatched brace in fields segment in strict mode
throws on duplicate field names at the same brace level in strict mode
tests.note: A bare field and a nested field group sharing one name yield duplicate sibling keys in every decoded row

tests.name: throws on entry row count mismatch with keyed header length
throws on entry row cell count not matching the leaf-field count
throws on an entry row with no cells after the entry key
tests.note: A bare entrykey: has zero cells, and a fields segment always declares at least one leaf field (§9.5)

tests.name: throws on keyed header without a fields segment in strict mode
tests.note: The fields segment is required in keyed headers (§6)

tests.name: throws on keyed marker after the delimiter symbol in strict mode
tests.note: The keyed colon must immediately follow the length and precede the delimiter symbol: [2:|]

tests.name: throws on keyed marker with leading-zero length in strict mode
throws on whitespace before the keyed marker in strict mode
throws on explicit comma delimiter after the keyed marker in strict mode
tests.note: Comma is never written as a delimiter symbol; [N:] already declares it

tests.name: throws on inline content after a keyed header colon in strict mode
tests.note: A keyed header carries no inline entries (§6)

tests.name: throws on a line without an unquoted colon at entry depth in strict mode
throws on duplicate entry keys in strict mode
throws on a keyless keyed header as a list item in strict mode
tests.note: The keyless keyed form is valid only as the document's root header (§5, §9.5)

tests.name: throws on inner array item count not matching its declared length
throws on keyless array header in object field position
throws on keyless array header after a depth-0 field
tests.note: Keyless headers are valid only as the document's root header or as list items (§6)

tests.name: throws on keyless fields-bearing header as list item
throws on inline content after tabular header
tests.note: A fields-bearing header carries no inline content (§6); decoding the values as an inline array would silently drop the fields segment

tests.name: throws on inline content after root tabular header
